ls-prd.name: ls-prd
ls-prd.description: Produce compressed proto-epics across 3-8 features with scenario-driven acceptance criteria. Each feature section seeds automated epic expansion through the full Liminal Spec pipeline.

ls-arch.name: ls-arch
ls-arch.description: Produce a research-grounded Technical Architecture document that settles foundational technical decisions across 3-8 epics. Companion to the PRD — establishes the technical world that downstream tech designs inherit.

ls-epic.name: ls-epic
ls-epic.description: Write complete, traceable Epics using Liminal Spec methodology. Covers User Profile, Flows, Acceptance Criteria, Test Conditions, Data Contracts, and Story Breakdown.

ls-tech-design.name: ls-tech-design
ls-tech-design.description: Transform a Feature Spec into implementable Tech Design with architecture, interfaces, and test mapping. Validates the spec as downstream consumer.

ls-publish-epic.name: ls-publish-epic
ls-publish-epic.description: Publish a detailed epic as individual story files with full AC/TC detail and Jira section markers, and optionally a PO-friendly business epic with grouped ACs.

ls-current-docs.name: ls-current-docs
ls-current-docs.description: Reconstruct an evidence-bound current-state baseline from implemented code, tests, runtime surfaces, and historical specs so fresh agents can onboard without replaying every prior epic. Use this whenever the user needs to understand what an implemented system currently does, wants current-state docs after an epic ships, is onboarding onto a mature codebase whose older epics no longer describe the whole, or needs current functional + technical truth before writing the next spec.

ls-codex-impl.name: ls-codex-impl
ls-codex-impl.description: Orchestrate story-by-story implementation inside Codex with gpt-5.4 xhigh as the persistent orchestrator, Codex workers for implementation, and dual fresh verification from gpt-5.4 xhigh and claude sonnet 4.6 max. Use this whenever the user has published stories plus a tech design and test plan and wants Codex itself to run the implementation harness rather than Claude Code teams or a raw external CLI loop.

ls-team-impl.name: ls-team-impl
ls-team-impl.description: Orchestrate story-by-story implementation with agent teams and an external CLI model (Codex or Copilot). Opus teammates manage external model subagents for implementation and verification.

ls-team-impl-cc.name: ls-team-impl-cc
ls-team-impl-cc.description: Orchestrate story-by-story implementation with Claude Code agent teams. Sonnet implements with TDD methodology, Opus and Sonnet verify in fresh sessions with evidence-bound review. No external CLI required.

ls-team-spec.name: ls-team-spec
ls-team-spec.description: Orchestrate the full Liminal Spec pipeline with agent teams and external CLI verification. Procedural learning architect manages drafters and verifiers from orientation through published stories.
